title: Conflict-free work-tracking: UUIDv7 ids and a directory-as-tracker
problem_statement: When teammates on separate branches author issues, PRDs, or ADRs in the same Instance and push, the work-tracking set collides in git. Two structural causes: backlog.json is a single JSON array, so every appended issue fights the same closing bracket; and ids are locally-allocated sequential counters (EXEC-NNN, PRD-NNN, sequential ADRs), so two authors offline both grab the next number. Sprint files compound it — concurrent step_log appends contend for the same line. The pain is real and already on record: Sprint 4's retro logged 'Issue-id collision from parallel filing', and Sprint 6's logged a clerk hand-edit that left a trailing comma in backlog.json.
solution: Make work-tracking records self-identifying and stop storing them in monoliths. Every new record carries a UUIDv7 id, minted offline with no coordinator, so two branches never contend for the same identifier. backlog.json is replaced by one file per issue under .excn/issues/, named <uuid>-<slug>.json — the directory is the tracker, with deliberately no manifest (a list-of-all file would reintroduce the conflict). Two branches then never touch the same path. Format is guaranteed at a single choke point: a writeRecord helper is the exclusive write path for issues and sprints, minting the uuid, validating against the schema, and emitting canonical serialization; a thin PreToolUse channel guard denies raw writes to those homes so the helper can't be bypassed. Existing EXEC-NNN/PRD-NNN/sequential-ADR ids are grandfathered (schemas widen to accept legacy or UUIDv7); a one-time migration splits the existing backlog and retrofits sprint files. No server is needed — UUIDv7 removes the only thing a coordinator was ever for. Decisions recorded in ADR-0011; prior art in .excn/research/prior-art-shared-coordination-team-server.md.

user_stories.actor: teammate authoring issues on a feature branch
user_stories.want: to file new issues while a teammate on another branch files theirs
user_stories.benefit: both branches push and merge with no git conflict in the work-tracking set.

user_stories.want: a single sanctioned way to create and update issues and append sprint verdicts
user_stories.benefit: records are always schema-valid and canonically serialized, and a hand-edit can never corrupt the JSON.

user_stories.actor: Team Lead upgrading an existing Instance
user_stories.want: a one-time command that splits the legacy backlog and retrofits sprint files without rewriting history or losing data
user_stories.benefit: the Instance moves to the new layout safely and idempotently, recoverable via git if anything goes wrong.

user_stories.actor: anyone reading where the work stands
user_stories.want: the issue directory and the viewer to reflect the per-file layout, with records referenceable by short id prefix
user_stories.benefit: work history stays browsable and citable without a monolith or a running coordinator.

implementation_decisions.title: UUIDv7 self-minted ids
implementation_decisions.summary: Every new record's id is a UUIDv7, minted offline with no coordinator; the timestamp prefix keeps records time-sortable. Humans and agents reference a record by its 8-char short prefix, the way they already reference commit SHAs. The tidy sequential integer and 'EXEC-091..096' range shorthand are given up; chronological order survives, contiguous numeric ranges do not.

implementation_decisions.title: Directory is the tracker, no manifest
implementation_decisions.summary: backlog.json is replaced by one file per issue under the issues home, named <uuid>-<slug>.json with the slug required and frozen at create (the title may drift in-content; the filename never moves, since references key off the uuid). There is deliberately no manifest or index file — readers glob the directory. This amends ADR-0005's 'backlog.json is the single issue tracker'.

implementation_decisions.title: writeRecord helper — exclusive write path for issues and sprints
implementation_decisions.summary: A shared helper accepts a JSON record, validates it against the schema with a bundled ajv (never a host-root install), writes atomically, and emits canonical serialization. For issues it mints the UUIDv7 and rejects a caller-supplied id (id immutable; other fields stay updatable). For sprints it emits canonical-sentinel form (accreting arrays last, a constant sentinel key dead-last) and exposes a step_log append mode so a gate adds one verdict without rewriting the file. This is the single format authority; it supersedes the scribe-assigns-ids role in ADR-0007 (scribe remains the owner, not the id authority).

implementation_decisions.title: CLI surface over the helper
implementation_decisions.summary: Agent-facing commands (to-execution issue create|update, to-execution sprint ...) wrap the helper. scribe, clerk, and gates call these instead of authoring JSON directly — closing the read-write gap the same way the Scaffolder did for the invariant layout.

implementation_decisions.title: One thin channel guard, not a content re-check
implementation_decisions.summary: Because the helper writes via the filesystem and never the agent Write tool, any raw Write/Edit to the issues or sprints home is by definition a bypass. A single PreToolUse guard — modeled on progress-location-guard, a hard deny whose reason redirects to the CLI — denies those raw writes and needs no uuid/shape/schema logic. It subsumes the monolith-shape ban and the uuid/serialization checks; format is guaranteed by construction at the helper. A new toggle joins the hooks-config features.

implementation_decisions.title: Grandfather legacy ids; widen the schemas
implementation_decisions.summary: Existing EXEC-NNN / PRD-NNN / sequential-ADR ids are kept as-is; issue and PRD id-patterns widen to accept legacy or UUIDv7. New records always mint UUIDv7. No historical record is rewritten.

implementation_decisions.title: One-time migration command, distinct from migrate
implementation_decisions.summary: A separate command splits backlog.json into per-issue files (preserving legacy ids) and retrofits existing sprint files to canonical-sentinel form. It resolves the repo root by absolute path, is idempotent (no-op once split), never clobbers an existing per-issue file, and verifies every write re-reads schema-valid before deleting the monolith. It does not ride under migrate, which stays location-only per ADR-0008. All inputs are committed, so a botched run is git-recoverable.

implementation_decisions.title: PRDs and ADRs stay manually authored
implementation_decisions.summary: PRDs (JSON, by execution-to-prd) and ADRs (markdown, by the grill) are low-churn, already one-file-per-record, and not the conflict hot path. They are created manually and not channel-guarded; each mints its own UUIDv7 by skill/grill instruction.

implementation_decisions.title: process-adherence is the set-level backstop
implementation_decisions.summary: The issue-set schema/integrity check folds into process-adherence at the lifecycle transition (orphans, references resolve, a manual sequential-id regression in PRDs/ADRs) — for every artifact type. No adversarial Adherence Agent per issue file; the channel already guarantees per-file format.

implementation_decisions.title: Viewer reads the per-file directory
implementation_decisions.summary: The status-page viewer globs the issues directory instead of reading backlog.json; the time-sortable UUIDv7 prefix yields creation order for free.

testing_decisions: writeRecord helper: mints a valid UUIDv7 on create; rejects a caller-supplied id; preserves the id and allows other-field updates; emits canonical-sentinel serialization for sprints; step_log append adds one entry without rewriting siblings; write is atomic (no partial file on failure); filename is <uuid>-<slug>.json.

Migration command: idempotent (second run is a no-op); splits every backlog issue into its own file preserving legacy ids; retrofits sprint files to canonical-sentinel; verifies all writes schema-valid before deleting the monolith; never clobbers an existing per-issue file; resolves repo root by absolute path.
Channel guard: denies a raw Write/Edit to the issues and sprints homes with a redirect reason; allows helper-originated writes; leaves PRD/ADR and other paths untouched.
out_of_scope: Any shared coordination server or ID-minting service — UUIDv7 removes the need; the write-capable viewer server (EXEC-056) is now an orthogonal live-editing UX feature, not conflict machinery.
Rewriting historical EXEC-NNN/PRD-NNN/ADR ids to UUIDv7 — grandfathered, not migrated.
Splitting sprint files into per-entry records — sprints stay one file with the sentinel trick (one team, one branch; low real concurrency).
Channel-guarding PRDs and ADRs — they stay manually authored.
An opt-in, config-gated remote-sync cron that pulls others' issues mid-session — parked as a future idea (see notes).
notes: Provenance: Epic Grill on 2026-06-15 (requirements + design pass); decisions recorded in ADR-0011 (alignment PASS x2); prior art in .excn/research/prior-art-shared-coordination-team-server.md.
Open at breakdown: the one-time migration command's name (e.g. split-issues) and the channel-guard feature default (on vs off in the Template until proven on the dogfood Instance).

Future idea (out of scope, not filed): a config-gated read-only issue-drift notice or an explicit to-execution sync — the uuid file-split makes pulling others' issues from main conflict-free by construction; the only hazard is mutating the working tree mid-session, so the safe form is read-only or explicit, never a sneaky working-tree write.
Src-First: all of this lands in src/ first, then the root Instance mirrors. The PRD itself is PRD-011 (sequential, grandfathered — authored before the cutover).
